instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar, low fat. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe veggitos recipe.
output.directions:
1. Mix nutritional yeast and seasonings in a small bowl or shaker and follow the directions below using the amount of seasoning that suits your taste.
2. "SQUASHITOS": Preheat oven to 200 degrees Thinly slice zucchini or squash (about 1/8 inch, a mandolin works great for this).
3. Place on a parchment lined baking sheet and mist lightly.
4. Sprinkle with seasoning and bake for 1-1.5 hours.
5. "KALE-ITOS": Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
6. Rinse, remove thick rib of several kale leaves and pat until very, very dry.
7. Tear into bite-size pieces.
8. Massage all sides with very light coating of olive oil.
9. Bake in a single layer until crispy, about 7-10 minutes.
10. Some leaves may bake sooner, so check at 7 minutes and remove the crispiest chips every few minutes so they don’t burn.
11. Sprinkle with seasoning once removed from the oven.
12. "BRUSSELITOS": Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
13. Cut of thick stem of several brussels sprouts, and let the outer leaves fall off until you reach the thick center (center can be saved for other recipes).
14. Massage all sides with very light coating of olive oil.
15. Bake in a single layer until crispy, about 7-10 minutes.
16. Some leaves may bake sooner, so check at 10 minutes and remove the crispiest chips every few minutes so they don’t burn.
17. Sprinkle with seasoning once removed from the oven.
18. "JICAMITOS": Peel and thinly slice jicama into “chips” No baking necessary, just sprinkle with seasoning and enjoy!.

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe very veggie turkey meatloaf recipe.
output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees and spray a small loaf pan with cooking spray or coat with canola oil.
2. In a large skillet, warm oil on medium heat.
3. Sautée onions, carrot, spinach, mushrooms and garlic all together until slightly soft, about 2-3 minutes.
4. Cool about 5 minutes in a medium sized bowl.
5. Add remaining ingredients and mix well using your hands.
6. Place in loaf pan and form into a loaf shape.
7. Cook about 1 hour until slightly browned and cooked through. 1-2 tablespoon optional toppings (like sweet and sour sauce, BBQ sauce or ketchup) can be spread on top and baked for the last 5-10 minutes, or served on the side for dipping.

output.description: A liver-safe sweet potato muffins recipe.

ingredients.name: sweet potatoes, peeled, boiled, and mashed

output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
2. Peel and cube 2-3 large sweet potatoes.
3. Put potatoes in a pot with water.
4. Bring to a boil and cook until fork tender.
5. Drain potatoes and mash with a potato masher.
6. In one bowl mix flour, cinnamon, baking powder, and salt.
7. In another bowl mix egg, sugar, oil, applesauce, and 2 cups sweet potato mash.
8. Pour wet ingredients into dry and mix until evenly combined.
9. Line muffin tin with 12 cups and fill cups ⅔ full.
10. Bake for 20-25 minutes until toothpick comes out clean.

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe turkey-veggie burgers recipe.
output.directions:
1. Pulse onion, garlic, carrot, and spinach and herbs in a food processor until fine (a mini food processor works great for this job).
2. Heat olive oil in a medium skillet on medium heat.
3. Add ground vegetable mixture and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
4. Cool slightly.
5. In a large bowl add mixture to turkey and remaining ingredients and combine.
6. Form into patties.
7. If patties seem too wet, add additional breadcrumbs.
8. Grill on a well oiled grill (or foil that has been sprayed) over a medium flame about 7 minutes on each side.
9. Make sure inside is well cooked.
10. Serve on a bun, wrap, pita or on on top of a salad with your favorite condiments.

output.description: A liver-safe warm potato salad recipe.

ingredients.name: sweet potatoes, peeled, cut into half inch cubes
white potatoes, cut into half inch cubes

output.directions:
1. Put sweet and white potatoes in a pot and cover with water.
2. Bring to a boil.
3. Cook until tender (about 15 minutes), then drain and place in large bowl.
4. Rinse and drain the chickpeas.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons of the olive oil in a skillet on medium-high.
6. Cook onions, carrot and ginger, and bell peppers stirring occasionally until tender, about 5 minutes.
7. Add chickpeas, raisins, cumin, turmeric, cinnamon, pepper, salt and cayenne (optional).
8. Stir until fragrant about 2 minutes.
9. Add veggies and spices to the potatoes, along with lemon juice, parsley and remaining olive oil.
10. Stir until combined, breaking up the potatoes slightly.
11. Adjust seasoning to taste and serve warm or cold.

output.description: A liver-safe pear & cranberry crisp recipe.
output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a large bowl, combine pears, lemon juice, cornstarch, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, vanilla, 1/4 teaspoon salt, nutmeg, and cranberries.
3. Pour into prepared 9-inch pie plate.
4. To make the crumble topping, combine oat flour, rolled oats, light brown sugar, cinnamon, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and 1 teaspoon cinnamon in medium bowl.
5. Cut in vegan butter with 2 forks large clumps form.
6. Cover fruit with crumble topping and bake for 30-40 minutes.
7. Check for topping to be crisp and browned!.

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar, low fat. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe shipwreck salmon with citrus glaze recipe.
output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
2. Place fish on a baking sheet skin side down and lightly salt.
3. Mix grated lemon rind, fresh lemon juice, honey, chili powder, ground cumin, red pepper flakes and juice concentrate in a saucepan and before heating, brush the fish with the orange mixture.
4. Place orange and lemon slices on top and bake until flaky (about 15 minutes for small fillets and 20-25 for larger, thicker piece).
5. While the fish is baking, place the saucepan with orange mixture on high heat until boiling.
6. Reduce heat to medium and cook about 2 minutes until reduced by almost half.
7. When salmon is done (should be cooked but still moist on the inside), drizzle with this sauce and garnish with chopped parsley.

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.recipe_name: Nimo'S Chicken And Butternut Squash Stew
output.description: A liver-safe nimo's chicken and butternut squash stew recipe.

ingredients.name: 4-5 chicken thighs, skin removed and fat trimmed
mushrooms, sliced and given a rough chop
large handful green beans, trimmed, cut in thirds

output.directions:
1. Place chicken, carrot, onions, mushrooms, garlic and thyme sprigs in a large soup pot or Dutch oven.
2. Fill pot with cold water so that it covers top of the chicken and veggies.
3. Bring to a boil with salt, pepper and turmeric.
4. Lower heat to a simmer until chicken is tender and falls easily off the bone, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
5. Discard any fat you can skim off the top.
6. Take the chicken out and pull off the bone, shredding the chicken as you go.
7. Return the shredded chicken to the pot and discard bones and other waste.
8. Add butternut squash and bring back to a boil.
9. Cook on low until squash is falling apart, about 45 minutes.
10. Use the back of wooden spoon to break apart the squash so that it incorporates into the broth.
11. Add green beans (or spinach) and cook an additional 10 minutes.
12. Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.

nutrition.productName: nimo's chicken and butternut squash stew

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar, low fat. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe kale and brussels sprouts saute recipe.

ingredients.name: Brussels sprouts, trimmed, halved, and thinly sliced

output.directions:
1. Wash, dry, and rough chop (removing large stems) one bundle of kale.
2. Peel and thinly slice 2 shallots.
3. Peel and mince 4 cloves of garlic.
4. Prepare shaved Brussels sprouts by trimming off and discarding the end, slice sprouts in half, then thinly slice each half into shavings similar to cabbage for coleslaw.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a large fry pan or wok.
6. Add shallot and garlic and saute until soft, without browning.
7. Add Brussels sprouts, stirring continuously for about 10 minutes.
8. Add kale and continue to stir mixture while kale wilts.
9. Season with a teaspoon of sea salt, cracked black pepper to taste, and 1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes.
10. Pour 1 cup of vegetable stock over kale, stir, and cover for 5 minutes.
11. Uncover, continue to saute until most of the liquid is evaporated.

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe beet, carrot, and arugula salad recipe.
output.directions:
1. Wash and dry produce.
2. Heat a small pot of salted water to boiling on high.
3. While heating, peel beets.
4. Cut in half then slice into half inch pieces.
5. Add to the boiling water and cook for 18 minutes.
6. Peel carrots, halve lengthwise, cut into 2 inch long pieces on an angle, set aside.
7. Peel and mince garlic, set aside.
8. Zest lemon and set aside 2 tsp.
9. Juice the lemon and put juice in a bowl with the chopped onion.
10. Once beets are cooked and drained, add them to the lemon juice mixture and toss.
11. Season with salt and pepper and allow to marinate for at least 5 minutes.
12. While beets are cooking, heat oil in a medium pan.
13. Add the carrots and cook, stirring frequently 4-6 minutes until lightly browned.
14. Add ¼ c water, season with salt and pepper, and keep stirring until carrots soften and water cooks out.
15. Add butter and garlic, allow garlic to cook.
16. Add lemon zest, marinated beets including the marinade liquid and cook a few more minutes.
17. Remove from heat, toss with fresh arugula and serve right away.

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe friteeni frittatas recipe.

ingredients.name: peeled and finely diced sweet potatoes

output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
2. In a medium sized bowl beat eggs, milk, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/8 teaspoon pepper, and set aside.
3. Grease two muffin tins with butter or oil.
4. In a large skillet, heat olive oil on medium heat.
5. Cook onions 2-3 minutes until slightly soft.
6. Add sweet potatoes and cook about 4-6 minutes until soft.
7. Add Swiss chard and rosemary, and cook another 4 minutes or so, until greens are all wilted.
8. Add additional salt and pepper to taste.
9. Fill the bottom of each muffin tin with about a tablespoon of cooked vegetables.
10. Spread the vegetables evenly until all 24 cups are filled.
11. Place 1-2 tablespoons of egg mixture into each of the cups, nearly covering the vegetables.
12. Bake about 10-12 minutes.
13. Take tins out and sprinkle a pinch of parmesan cheese on each little frittata.
14. Place back in the oven for another approximately 5 minutes until cooked through (watch carefully so they don’t brown too much).
15. Remove each frittata with a silicone spatula and serve warm.

output.description: A liver-safe italian lemon loaf recipe.
output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees, grease loaf pan with spray or olive oil.
2. Combine all dry ingredients in a bowl.
3. Then add water, olive oil, yogurt, lemon juice, and melted vegan butter and quickly combine all the ingredients to a smooth batter, being careful not to over mix.
4. Pour batter into greased loaf pan and bake for 35-45 minutes or until a skewer comes out clean.
5. Let cool and flip loaf pan over onto a long plate for easy removal.
6. Mix glaze until creamy and spread over cake.
7. Let firm before slicing, then enjoy!.

output.description: A liver-safe pumpkin breakfast muffins recipe.
output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 350.
2. Coat muffin tins with a fine mist of oil or line with paper cups Combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt and spices in a medium sized bowl.
3. In a mixer on low combine brown sugar, eggs, oil, milk, yogurt and honey until well blended.
4. Add water and pumpkin and combine until smooth.
5. Add flour mixture to pumpkin mixture slowly on low until just combined.
6. Fill cups 3/4 full and bake for 23-28 minutes until toothpick comes out clean.

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe bean lasagna recipe.
output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Heat oil on medium heat in a large skillet.
3. Sautee onions, garlic and pepper until soft.
4. Empty mixture into a large bowl and mix in beans, tomatoes, salsa, chili powder, and cumin, mixing well.
5. In smaller bowl combine ricotta, egg, and 1/4 cup parmesan cheese.
6. Spray a 9×13 glass baking dish with cooking spray.
7. Place about a cup of the tomato mixture in the bottom of the pan.
8. Cut tortillas in half and make a layer of tortillas over the tomatoes (using the straight sides up against the sides of the pan).
9. Top with half of the remaining tomato mixture.
10. Spoon half of ricotta mixture over the top, spreading carefully.
11. Sprinkle with 1/2 cup colby jack cheese.
12. Put another layer of tortillas on top.
13. Spread remainder of tomato mixture, then remainder of ricotta mixture, then 1/2 cup colby jack.
14. Place one last layer of tortillas on top and sprinkle with remaining parmesan and colby jack cheese.
15. Cover and bake for about 50 minutes until cheese is bubbly.
16. Take foil off and bake an additional 5-10 minutes.
17. Let stand 10-15 minutes before serving.

instruction: Generate a liver-safe recipe that is low sodium, low sugar. Health score must be at least 50/100. Format ingredients as a JSON array with quantity, measurement, and name fields. Format directions as numbered steps separated by newlines.

output.description: A liver-safe roasted vegetables with quinoa recipe.

ingredients.name: fresh basil leaves, cut into strips

output.directions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
2. Toss eggplant, peppers, squash, zucchini, broccoli, cauliflower, brussel sprouts, red onion, and garlic with 1/3 cup olive oil, 1 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon pepper on a large baking sheet.
3. Roast for 40 minutes, until browned, turning once halfway through.
4. While vegetable are roasting, cook 1 cup of quinoa according to package instructions.
5. Make dressing by whisking together 1/3 cup olive oil, 1/3 cup lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper and set aside.
6. Transfer cooked quinoa to a large serving bowl and add the lemon dressing.
7. When vegetables are done, scrape them and all juices from the pan into the serving bowl with the quinoa.
8. Top with fresh basil.
9. Serve with feta and pine nuts as optional toppings.

output.description: A liver-safe chicken curry recipe.
output.directions:
1. Heat oil over medium heat in a large skillet.
2. Fry onions until soft and just starting to brown, about 2-3 minutes.
3. Add ketchup, curry powder, chili powder, cinnamon, and turmeric until spices are slightly roasted.
4. Add garlic and ginger and cook an additional minute.
5. Add chicken and cook until slightly brown.
6. Add salt, pepper and coconut milk.
7. Simmer in coconut milk and spices for and additional 4-6 minutes.
8. Add additional salt to taste.

output.description: A liver-safe classic guacamole recipe.
output.directions:
